name: Exam 1

exercises.label: Reading Part 4
exercises.heading: Part 1: PERSONALITY
exercises.description: WHAT DOES YOUR PERSONALITY SAY ABOUT YOU?{v} Personality tests are an extremely popular way of finding out what type of person you are. There are several different types of personality tests, a few of which are extremely famous and are even used by companies when deciding who to hire for a job! While different personality tests have special names for each personality type, one of the most popular and simple strategies is to split personalities up into 4 different types: A (the Director), B (the Socializer), C (the Thinker), and D (the Supporter). None of these personality types is better than the next, but each of them has advantages and disadvantages.{v} If you are goal-oriented, want to achieve big things, like to be in charge of your life, and are extremely proactive then you are probably a type A personality. The great thing about this type of person is that they are ambitious and will work hard to pursue their goals. However, there are some downfalls. Type A personalities tend to get stressed easily, especially when they feel like they are not making progress and they almost always have a sense of urgency. This can cause them to be interpreted as aggressive, even though it’s just because they have such high goals. Because they like being in charge, people with a type-A personality are often called control-freaks which makes other people feel uncomfortable around them. Finally, because they are always focused on the big picture, they can easily overlook important details! {v} Everyone has that friend who is extremely outgoing, always full of energy, and seems to live life at an extremely fast pace - that’s what type B personalities are like. This type of person loves to have fun and spend time with people, in fact, they’re really good at connecting with others and are sometimes called “the life of the party”. But while they can be great fun to spend time with, they tend to be attention-seekers, have a very short attention span, and get bored really easily. After all, since they want everyone to like them they have to be as interesting as possible. A primary negative aspect of this personality type is the need for approval, so they place too much value in other people giving them praise. {v} The next personality type, type C, is on the other side of the scale when compared to type A. If you are a very calm person who has no problem taking your type and tolerating people, this might be you. People with a type C personality have a very logical way of thinking and are extremely focused-on detail, many times they’re referred to as perfectionists. One of the great things about this type of person is that they are very reliable and will keep their word, however, sometimes they are very skeptical and have a “glass-half-empty” perspective which means they don’t tend to have very high expectations. Lastly, a lot of type C personalities are so focused on details that they are indecisive, so they’re not the best person when it comes to making a decision. {v} Having a consistent routine and organized schedule can be nice for anyone, but people with type D personalities live for organization. This type of person likes to take things slow and live in a controlled and predictable environment where things are the same every day and they can focus on completing one task after another. They are normally very caring and sincere, but they tend to keep to themselves as they’re not very sociable. A disadvantage to this type of personality is that they can be very sensitive and it’s easy to hurt their feelings or make them uncomfortable by messing with their daily routine. {v} Sometimes, when people are trying to figure out what personality type they are it turns out that they are actually a mix of 2! When someone exhibits half the traits from one personality type and half the traits from another, they are called a type X personality. This name is also used for someone who has bits and pieces from all 4 types, making them like a personality chameleon! Having a type X personality means that they do not belong in a single category, but this is not something typical to find. {v} Each personality type is extremely complex, but normally, by taking a test or paying close attention to what you like and dislike, the type of life you prefer to live, and the way you react to different situations can give you a general idea of what type of personality you are.

exercises.heading: Part 2: Jobs for Graduates
exercises.description: PARAGRAPH A. {v} More and more students in the United States are faced with the fact that, while attending a high-tier university may be their dream, it is financially unrealistic. American universities can charge tuition that can sum up to as much as $50,000 a year. For an average, middle-class citizen, these numbers are hard to swallow. Therefore, it is quite logical when students start looking into less financially imposing educational methods. It is true that scholarships are scattered all over the US education system being awarded to students for various achievements such as academic or athletic, but statistics show that only 1.5% of students are able to obtain a full-ride scholarship and 18.8% can secure that half of their tuition is paid for in scholarships. Needless to say, betting one’s chances on a university scholarship may not be the most effective plan of action. {v} PARAGRAPH B. {v}  While some students might choose to take a gap year in which they can save money for the daunting university fees ahead, other students take a more creative approach that still allows for them to advance in their studies while not having to dig themselves into a hole of debt. Community colleges are sometimes frowned upon and viewed as providing students with a lower level of education, however, the fact of the matter is that many extremely successful students have chosen this path. There are many advantages to attending a community college, a big one being the extremely low tuition price. Additionally, most students who enroll at a community college are able to commute every day, which allows them to save money on accommodation costs. It is true that many students wish to complete their university career with the name of a well-known and distinguished university carved onto their diploma. What many students don’t know is that more than half of the community college students are able to transfer to a more prominent university after two years, allowing them to complete their degree with an illustrious-looking diploma. {v} PARAGRAPH C. {v} Having university behind them, students then face the subduing reality that they then have to enter the work world to take everything that they have learned and put it into practice. For some students, this is not challenging at all as they may have interned at a firm or made connections throughout their university career. Those who have chosen to work in the medical field are highly qualified and on-demand when they finally are able to close the books that they’ve had their heads stuck in for years and years. Unfortunately, more than half of college graduates truly struggle to find jobs out of university and many of them end up working a job in a career that they did not major in. Studies have shown that these students do not lack a level of employability but have not been properly trained in what job hunting involves. Connections are everything when it comes to finding a job, and recent graduates must be creative when considering this. On top of that, social media and the world wide web provide students with a plethora of information that can lead them to job vacancies. {v} PARAGRAPH D. {v} Sadly, picking up the pieces that lead to such a large percentage of unemployed recent graduates is not a challenging task. Plunging into the workforce is difficult, but those looking for jobs must put every ounce of their being into preparing. An important factor includes the way they interact with a potential future boss, whether that be during a face-to-face interaction, or simply over email. Recent university graduates are guilty of being too casual, therefore causing the interviewer to believe that they are not serious or cut-out for the job at hand. Furthermore, recent graduates tend to border on the side of being unrealistic about the jobs they can be hired for with minimal experience, or they do not give themselves enough credit and accept the first opportunity they have. Maintaining a balance of these two extremes is necessary to be successful in finding a job that is both appropriate and satisfying. {v} PARAGRAPH E. {v} Being a student does not necessarily have to end the moment the tassel on that graduation cap shifts to the other side. While many individuals choose to jump head first into the working world, others have set a different type of goal for themselves. Higher education is sometimes necessary when it comes to certain careers and some students feel that they want to continue their education in order to further their knowledge and potential in their future careers. However, it is not uncommon for a student to pursue higher education whilst working a job. In fact, many incorporations run programs which allow for their employees to work part-time while pursuing a masters degree, ultimately enhancing their value as an asset for the company itself. No matter the motive behind it, many people find higher education to be both beneficial on an educational level but also allows for further personal growth. {v} PARAGRAPH F. {v} Regardless of the fact that generation after generation have been steered to follow the path of education through a university education, there are other ways to achieve one's career goals while not holding any type of university diploma. Some may scoff at the idea of apprenticeships, but others argue that they are the most practical way to grow into one's dream career from an early age. An apprenticeship is not for those who are unsure of their passion, yet it brings up very solid arguments when it comes to financial figures. Most apprenticeships allow young people to earn money as they are gaining knowledge in their trade of choice. Finally, when they complete their training they can easily transition into the working world, choosing to take that knowledge in whatever direction they choose. Both a university degree and an apprenticeship are ways to achieve one’s career goals, both bringing very different factors to the table.

exercises.heading: Part 3: Sports injuries
exercises.description: PARAGRAPH A.{v} Participation in sports is highly encouraged all around the world as scientific studies lead us to believe that there are numerous health benefits. In the UK, 63% of adult men and 58% of adult women participate in some type of form of exercise on a weekly basis. This could vary from an activity as simple as walking, to team sports like football or basketball and might even extend to some sports that acquire technical training and experience such as fencing. One's participation in sport throughout the adult life hinges greatly upon one's subjection to sports as a child. It is estimated that almost 90% of children in the UK are enrolled or attend an organized sports group or team. Due to this fact, while many individuals choose to reduce their amount of exercise as they age, others maintain this healthy habit throughout their adult life. {v} PARAGRAPH B. {v}While sports do promote a healthy life, they do not come without their fair share of dangers and cautionary tales. Most athletes, of any sort, will experience an injury at one point or another. Sports injuries can be both painful and discouraging, even at a recreational level, as well as causing large hurdles in fitness goals and health. One of the most common injuries found affects the knee and is known as patellofemoral syndrome. This injury takes place when someone falls onto their knee, forcing the patella to shift away from its intended groove. In turn, there is an imbalance of weight distribution which can cause pain or further injury. Another frequent injury occurs in the shoulder area and might arise in the form of a dislocation, misalignment, strain or sprain. This may come about while participating in many sports as the shoulders are considered to be the weakest joint in the upper body. A third injury found in many athletes is that of sciatica. Athletes who suffer from sciatica are those that spend time in a flexed forward position, causing pain to spike down their back and through their legs, sometimes even reaching their feet. {v} PARAGRAPH C. {v} When faced with a sports injury there is a classic procedure that young athletes should be educated in from an early age. An acronym is used to help people remember it: PRICE. Protection is the first step when faced with an injury. If an injury is not appropriately protected, it could worsen, ultimately resulting in something far more serious. An essential factor to recovery is Rest. After an injury, an athlete must take the time to give their body the chance to recover by stopping all activity that might irritate their injury. A general rule of thumb is that an athlete should not participate in any vigorous activity for at least a week after all pain has subsided. Ice should be applied to injuries, even if there is not necessarily visible swelling. Applying the ice directly on the injured area is not recommended so there should be at least a thin cloth between the skin and the ice pack. It is terribly important to apply pressure to the injury, especially if there is possibility of inflammation or swelling. This step goes by the name Compression. Finally, an injured athlete should do their best to Elevate the injury, this forces the fluid to drain away from the damage which can promote the reduction of swelling. {v} PARAGRAPH D.{v} More likely than not, an athlete will feel restless and impatient when dealing with an injury and will either tell themselves they are ready prematurely, or seek out additional ways to speed their recovery. Unfortunately, one cannot rush the body and its healing process, but modern science has provided the option of working with a physical therapist as a way to strengthen one's body even amidst the healing process of an injury. At the end of the day, however, an injury may be just as taxing on the mind as it is on the body whilst impatiently waiting for the human body to work its magic. {v} PARAGRAPH E.{v} Easing back into the sports world after having spent time in recovery can be a difficult and discouraging process. One's body will have lost strength and mobility, causing an athlete to feel weak and dismayed by their lack of level. Additionally, one must deal with mental recovery, which can be just as challenging as any physical recovery. In said situation, one must realize that anguish and frustration are normal parts of recovery and are not things to shove away. However, within these feelings, maintaining a positive attitude is what will push an athlete through to the other side. Unfortunately, many athletes are faced with the “what if” or “if only” situation in which they replay their moment of injury in their head, visualizing a world in which they were never injured at all. This can delay their progress as the only option is to focus on the now and future and never the past. Lastly, consistency is key as recovery depends on slowly easing the body back to where the athlete was before, or even further! {v} PARAGRAPH F.{v}Injuries are a painful and tragic part of being an athlete. Sometimes they may not be the source of a huge roadblock, but in other situations they can change the course of an athletes relationship to their sport. While injury treatment is important to practise, the best type of treatment is reducing the chances of injury in the first place. As a general guideline, stretching and increasing flexibility will reduce the chances of injury and promote the speed of recovery. More and more athletes are recommended to engage in activities such as yoga or pilates to increase their flexibility. Investing in the correct gear that is not only appropriate for the sport, but also suitable for the individual can also promote safety while performing. Finally, learning proper technique with the help of experienced coaches or trainers will not only reduce the likelihood of injury, but allow one to be a better athlete in their target field!
exercises.label: Reading Part 4

questions.title: 1. What point does the writer make about participation in sports? {x}
answers: A. It is extremely normal for exercise to reduce with age.
B. Those who exercise as a kid will probably continue as they grow older.
C. Not even people are exercising on a daily basis.
D. There are more benefits to sports that require technical skills.

questions.title: 2. What does the acronym PRICE stand for? {x}
answers: A. Protect, Rest, Ice, Compress and Elevate
B. Protection, Rest, Ice, Compress and Elevation
C. Protection, Rest, Ice, Compression and Elevate

questions.title: 3. Some athletes may endanger themselves post injury by {x}
answers: A. Thinking they are ready before they are
B. Being physical therapy too soon

questions.title: 4.The key factor to recovery is {x}
answers: A. Carefully analyzing the situation that caused the injury so as not to repeat it
B. Learning to push past any anger or frustration that arises
C. Keeping up a forward-looking style of thinking
D. Listening carefully to the guidelines of those who have had similar experiences

questions.title: 5. Which is the best way to avoid injury at the outset? {x}
answers: A. Implement consist stretching exercises into your lifestyle
B. Never forget to warm up and cool down before exercise
C. Invest in learning the correct technique for your activities

title: Who Are You?
description: Explore personality, society, and self-expression — challenge your thinking with topics that go deeper than the surface.
